title: Evangelion 222 BD DVD
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 2.22, BD/DVD [NO SPOILERS]             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Mar 18, 2009 2:11 pm The german company which bought the license for Evangelion 1.01 is already negotiating for the DVD release of Evangelion 2.0. They will probably get it again. But anyway. They there already told that the earliest date for the DVD release is January 2010 . So it won´t take that long this time. I expect a Japanese release in decembre 2009.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Mar 18, 2009 2:45 pm Too early.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:05 pm DVD's take longer in Japan then they do in the USA. Don't expect a DVD like until Q2 or maybe Q3 of 2010.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:07 pm don´t tell me. this is what studio khara or whoever sells the rights told to universum/ufa anime, which is the german distributer...

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:23 pm Any source on this unexpectedly soon titbit of information?

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:28 pm master_lloyd wrote: Any source on this unexpectedly soon titbit of information?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:32 pm I trust him fine. It's the previous posters who've been dismissive.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Mar 18, 2009 3:36 pm master_lloyd wrote: I trust him fine. It's the previous posters who've been dismissive.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Mar 18, 2009 4:02 pm Well Film 1.0 came out on DVD in Q2 of 2008 and it's release date was over two months later in the year than this new film. My estimate for a DVD release in Japan is late January/February of 2010. But who knows, the Market is a weird business. The new Eureka Seven movie goes to DVD two months after it's theatrical release. It's unpredictable when films hit the home market sometimes.

[]:             Quote Post by Marzan » Thu Apr 02, 2009 1:44 pm At the current rate, German speakers will be able to have Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 before we get to see an english version. Oh well, patience is needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Thu Apr 02, 2009 1:55 pm Marzan wrote: At the current rate, German speakers will be able to have Evangelion 1.0 and 2.0 before we get to see an english version. Oh well, patience is needed.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Apr 02, 2009 2:50 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: i wonder why is that?

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Apr 02, 2009 2:56 pm Ze Germans haff vays of procuring zeir anime qvuickly.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Thu Apr 02, 2009 3:36 pm master_lloyd wrote: Ze Germans haff vays of getting zeir anime qvuickly.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Thu Apr 02, 2009 3:58 pm Apologies, but at least they seem to be making up for it.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Thu Apr 02, 2009 4:06 pm Yeah... But see it like this: You still have the chance of getting 1.11 right away instead - we'd have to buy it again (if it gets here at all). And who knows how often 2.0 will be redone... XP

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Thu Apr 02, 2009 5:35 pm I don't see why we wouldn't get 1.11.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Thu Apr 02, 2009 7:12 pm cuz some people would buy 1.0, then 1.01 then 1.11

[]:             Quote Post by The Invincible Shinji » Thu Apr 02, 2009 7:55 pm Axell wrote: cuz some people would buy 1.0, then 1.01 then 1.11

[]:             Quote Post by bryan2369 » Fri Apr 03, 2009 7:14 pm Why the Hell can't there be one universal standard?

Article: Evangelion 222 BD DVD
 Well Film 1.0 came out on DVD in Q2 of 2008 and it's release date was over two months later in the year than this new film. My estimate for a DVD release in Japan is late January/February of 2010. But who knows, the Market is a weird business. The new Eureka Seven movie goes to DVD two months after it's theatrical release. It's unpredictable when films hit the home market sometimes.
